name: lever high row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and foot platform to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the foot platform.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and engage your core. Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position. conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
